LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-01-2023 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOANNE PARR'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS AND OR FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS CONTINUED TO BE HEARD ON THE COURT'S DISCOVERY CALENDAR (ROOM 301) AT 9:00 A.M. ON FEBRUARY 15, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DISCOVERY DEPT 302, AS TO (DATE) HEARING RE:PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS AND OR FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER - PARTIES STIPULATE TO JPT. DISCUSSION HELD. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION TO QUASH GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORD FORM TO THE COURT AT SBORROWMAN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: R SAHATJIAN; NOT REPORTED =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Feb-17-2023 Continued To Sep-25-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And Or For Protective Order
Off Calendar Feb-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty Michael R. Bracamontes, Esq. of BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, P.C. (Tele: 415-835-6777) for Plaintiff

Atty. Catherine A. Salah, Esq. of GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (Tele: 415-986-5900) for Defendant.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOANNE PARR Plaintiffs Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And Or For Protective Order.

The parties to the motion stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem. The pro tem judge and counsel for the parties to the motion authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter is discussed.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Motion to quash GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. In general the subpoenas are permissible, but the confusion over whether client lists are subject to the subpoenas suggests that the parties' meet-and-confer efforts were deficient. To limit further disputes, the motion is quash is denied for all categories of documents in the exclusive control of the third-party subpoena recipient, which means that personnel documents should be produced. Categories of documents not in the exclusive control of the third-party need not be produced, which means that client lists need not be produced. The Court encourages the parties to meet and confer about a protective order but cannot impose one based on this record. Both requests for sanctions are DENIED. As set out above, the meet and confer efforts were deficient.

THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORD FORM TO THE COURT at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: R Sahatjian; Not Reported =(302/JPT)
